statement: Eric wants to rent the house for 20 days.

provenance.author: Claude (Phase B round 2 agent)
provenance.author_note: Widening target: 'for N days' window-quantifier form not in v1 (v1's window_quantifier set was {each, every, per}).  The schema collapses 'for 20 days' to window_unit='day' with quantifier='per' and an implicit '20' multiplier.

statement: He can run 40 yards within 5 seconds.

provenance.author: Claude (Phase B round 2 agent)
provenance.author_note: Widening target: 'within N seconds' window form.  Mirrors v1 case ta-v1-0002 in shape but with the 'within' quantifier variant absent from v1.

statement: She studied for 2 hours on Wednesday and three times as long on Thursday.

provenance.author: Claude (Phase B round 2 agent)
provenance.author_note: Widening target: 'for N hours on <day>' composite.  Two day-windowed anchors (Wednesday, Thursday); the comparative inner 'three times as long' is not captured by the schema and is flagged for operator review.

provenance.author: Claude (Phase B round 2 agent)
provenance.author_note: Widening target: leading-clause 'Every week,' form (v1 covered only trailing 'every week' position).

statement: Lina sketched for 3 hours during the workshop.

provenance.author: Claude (Phase B round 2 agent)
provenance.author_note: Novel 'for N hours' widening (matches case 0036/0017 quantifier).

statement: The kettle boils within 2 minutes on full power.

provenance.author: Claude (Phase B round 2 agent)
provenance.author_note: Novel 'within N minutes' widening.  count_token='1' encodes the single boil event.

statement: Every month, the council reviews 4 applications.
Khalil practiced violin for 45 minutes during the recital prep.
The marathon runner crossed the finish line within 3 hours of the starting gun.

provenance.author: Claude (Phase B round 2 agent)
provenance.author_note: Novel 'within N hours' widening; count_token='1' encodes the single crossing event.

statement: Every year, the festival hosts 12 musicians on opening night.

provenance.author: Claude (Phase B round 2 agent)
provenance.author_note: Edge case: leading 'Every year,' with a year window — the longest window unit in the schema.
